name: Kaingang

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Kaingang (kain1272).

notes: Macro-Je language of southern Brazil, Latin orthography. The letters m/n/nh/g denote the nasal-consonant series whose oral and prestopped allophones ([m~b], [n~d], [ɲ~ɟ], [ŋ~ɡ]) are conditioned by adjacent vowel nasality and are not written; the nasal phonemic value is mapped. Note that g is the velar nasal, not a plosive, and s is realized as a palatal fricative. Vowel nasality is written with a tilde. Stress (tending to the final syllable) and the oral/prestopped allophony are left unencoded, raising the PER floor.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Kaingang (kain1272)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
